purpose: Deterministic NUMA and cache-locality lab contract for large-host scheduler planning without changing production scheduler policy or claiming hardware performance wins.

proof_lane.proof_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fifth_wave_numa_scheduler_locality_lab CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test fifth_wave_numa_scheduler_locality_lab_contract -- --nocapture
does_not_cover: does not prove scheduler performance improvement
does not change production scheduling policy
does not implement the fourth-wave governor
does not require real NUMA hardware
does not authorize local Cargo fallback

metric_catalog.interpretation: agent resident bytes divided by modeled per-agent budget
remote-node byte share only when topology confidence is high enough
cold or over-budget bytes to evict before considering production policy changes

fixture_catalog.summary: No real NUMA evidence is required; low topology confidence disables the remote-node penalty and keeps the projection portable.
High-confidence topology with elevated cache pressure routes cold spill-eligible bytes before production scheduler policy changes.
Critical cache plus per-agent budget overage yields red pressure and chooses the larger cold-byte target.

required_before_performance_claims: large-host run with explicit core count and memory size
NUMA topology capture attached to the benchmark receipt
scheduler fairness and cancellation-drain proof lanes green
comparison against current main with identical workload corpus
operator report that separates lab projection from observed hardware behavior
hardware_benchmark_plan.no_claim_when_absent: When no fresh hardware benchmark receipt is attached, this artifact may only prove deterministic lab projection behavior and no-claim boundaries.

failure_fixtures.expected_reason: remote RCH proof is required for closeout
artifact must not claim scheduler performance improvement
hardware claims require fresh large-host benchmark receipts
artifact must not change production scheduler policy

non_claims: does not prove scheduler performance improvement
does not change production scheduling policy
does not implement the fourth-wave governor
does not require real NUMA hardware
does not authorize local Cargo fallback
does not prove broad workspace health
does not create branches, worktrees, scratch clones, or non-main refs
closeout_requirements: Focused remote RCH proof for fifth_wave_numa_scheduler_locality_lab_contract must pass from the current committed main.
Closeout may cite this artifact only for deterministic NUMA/cache projection fixtures, metric catalog coverage, no-claim boundaries, and hardware-benchmark preconditions.
Any production scheduler policy, performance, no-regression, or release-readiness claim requires a separate proof lane and fresh benchmark receipt.
